titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.1

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, provosts, and all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. I order that our brethren of Melrose and all brethren of the same order shall be quit of toll and all other customs and what is owed by right even from all trading which they may have done for their own purposes wherever they may have come in the whole of my land, just as the charter of the lord king, William my father, bears witness and affirms. As witnesses: William del Bois, my chancellor; Earl Malcolm of Fife; William de Valognes; John of Maxwell. At Edinburgh on the second day of April.
